summary: All four bots completed one crypto-only scan because equities were closed. Only Scalper successfully placed trades after retrying symbol format and time_in_force, but race accounting appears broken because its open buys were recorded as an immediate -$150 PnL while Momentum failed on a symbol-mapping bug and the other two correctly held cash.
problems_found: PnL/accounting bug: Scalper shows net_pnl -150, net_spent 150, net_received 0 after two BUY orders, which means open position cost is being treated as realized loss instead of inventory/cash usage.

Ranking bug: Momentum is ranked #1 with 0 PnL and 0 trades while summary total_pnl is -150 and Scalper executed the only trades; race ranking logic is not aligned with actual performance or is favoring idle bots incorrectly.
Crypto symbol normalization inconsistency: market scans use BTC-USD/ETH-USD while broker accepted BTC/USD and ETH/USD only after manual retry; symbol mapping is not centralized.
Order retry logic is uneven across agents: Scalper retried symbol format and time_in_force automatically, but Momentum stopped after the first mapping failure instead of using the same fallback path.
Broker order parameter bug: initial crypto orders used unsupported time_in_force, causing avoidable failures before retry.
Truncated UI/log messages: Dip Buyer and Conservative 'Scan complete' explanations are cut off mid-sentence, indicating feed/storage/display length limits.
Weak agent design: Conservative is configured for a universe mostly outside the active race symbols and becomes non-participatory whenever equities are closed.
Weak race design: Dip Buyer had no possible setup from the available overnight symbol set and only scanned two green crypto assets, making it structurally unlikely to act.
Missing position-state data: export lacks current holdings, avg entry price, unrealized PnL, quantity/notional held, and cash remaining per bot, making post-race diagnosis incomplete.
Missing execution metadata: no broker order ids, order status lifecycle, rejected order payloads, or final filled quantities/prices are included.
No explicit realized vs unrealized PnL split in export, which likely contributed to the accounting confusion.
Summary quality issue: leader is reported as Momentum Rider despite no executed trades and tied 0 PnL among three bots; tie/leader logic is misleading.
recommended_changes: Fix portfolio accounting first: track cash, open positions, cost basis, realized PnL, and unrealized PnL separately so BUY orders reduce available cash without creating negative net_pnl.
Create a shared crypto symbol normalization and broker order builder used by every bot so scan symbols map automatically from feed format to tradable broker format before order placement.
Unify retry/fallback logic across all bots: on crypto order rejection, automatically retry normalized symbol and supported time_in_force='gtc' without each agent improvising.
Correct rank/leader calculation to use total equity or realized+unrealized PnL, with deterministic tie handling and no preference for inactive bots.
Expand export schema to include per-bot cash_remaining, open_positions, avg_entry, mark_price, unrealized_pnl, realized_pnl, order_attempts, rejection_reasons, and normalized_symbol_used.
Increase log/message storage or UI rendering limits so explanations are not truncated in latest_logs and live_feed.

Improve race participation rules: if equities are closed, either swap Conservative for a crypto-safe strategy or expand its allowed universe to include low-vol crypto when race mode includes crypto symbols.

Improve Dip Buyer inputs by scanning a wider tradeable universe or allowing it to remain inactive with an explicit 'no qualifying dips in universe' state rather than pretending competitiveness on a 2-symbol green-only scan.
Add validation tests for crypto orders to ensure correct symbol, time_in_force, notional sizing, and post-fill accounting before race results are persisted.
Add a race-level warning banner when the selected symbol set or market hours make one or more bots structurally unable to trade.
tests_to_run: Run a crypto-only 4-bot race after hours and verify all bots use normalized tradable symbols automatically without manual AI retry text.
Confirm a successful BUY decreases cash and creates an open position while net_pnl remains 0 until price changes or position closes.
Confirm realized_pnl and unrealized_pnl are both populated correctly after one open position and after a later sell.
Verify summary leader/rank ordering matches total equity or realized+unrealized PnL and handles ties correctly.
Force an initial bad crypto order parameter and ensure centralized retry converts to time_in_force='gtc' automatically for every bot.
Inspect exported JSON and verify presence of cash_remaining, open_positions, avg_entry, mark_price, realized_pnl, unrealized_pnl, and order rejection details.
Check that long 'Scan complete' explanations are fully visible in both latest_logs and live feed without truncation.
Run a race with equities closed and confirm the UI warns when a bot's strategy universe cannot participate.
next_prompt_for_replit: Fix the AI Brain portfolio accounting bug so open BUY orders no longer count as negative PnL. Update the race engine to track per-bot cash, open positions, cost basis, realized PnL, unrealized PnL, and total equity separately. A BUY should reduce available cash and increase position inventory, but net/realized PnL must stay unchanged until mark-to-market or sell. Add support for marking open positions to current market price so unrealized PnL is computed correctly. Then update race ranking/leader logic to rank bots by total equity or realized+unrealized PnL instead of treating spend as loss. Also extend the export JSON to include cash_remaining, open_positions, realized_pnl, unrealized_pnl, and total_equity for each bot. Finally add tests or at least deterministic validation code for this scenario: a bot with $250 buys $75 BTC and $75 ETH, receives fills, and should show cash_remaining=100, net_spent=150, realized_pnl=0, unrealized_pnl near 0 immediately after fill, total_equity about 250, and not rank below idle bots purely because it deployed capital.

summary: The race ran only one scan cycle per bot while equities were closed, so all bots evaluated only BTC and ETH. Scalper was the only bot that successfully converted crypto symbols and placed orders, but portfolio accounting appears broken because its open buys were immediately counted as -$150 PnL while Momentum failed on a symbol-mapping issue and the other two bots correctly held cash.
problems_found: Portfolio/PnL accounting bug: Scalper shows net_pnl = -150, net_spent = 150, net_received = 0 after two BUY orders with no sells, which implies cash outflow is being treated as realized loss instead of open position value.

Ranking bug: Momentum is ranked #1 with 0 PnL and 0 trades while Scalper is ranked #4 despite being the only bot to execute trades; if open positions are marked incorrectly, leaderboard logic is misleading.
Crypto symbol normalization is inconsistent: market data uses BTC-USD/ETH-USD while order placement requires BTC/USD or broker-specific tradable identifiers.
Momentum bot lacks the retry/fallback order logic that Scalper used, so it missed valid trades due to a recoverable symbol-format issue.
Order submission configuration bug: the system first used an unsupported crypto time_in_force before retrying with gtc.
Live/info messages are truncated for Dip Buyer and Conservative, indicating UI/export log clipping or message length handling issues.
Bots completed only 1 cycle and then stopped, suggesting the race may be terminating too early or not running continuous paper-trading loops.
Conservative bot scanned crypto even though its allowed universe is equities only; this is wasted cycle work and noisy logging.
No position-state data is exported for open holdings, average entry, quantity, market value, or unrealized PnL, making it impossible to verify whether buys actually settled correctly.
No broker order IDs, order status transitions, or rejection payload details are included, limiting diagnosis of execution failures.
Summary total_pnl = -150 likely inherits the same accounting bug and therefore misreports race performance.
No explicit account snapshot per bot after execution is shown, so available cash vs reserved cash vs position value cannot be audited.
recommended_changes: Fix portfolio accounting so BUY orders create/open positions and reduce available cash without affecting realized PnL; compute net_pnl from realized PnL plus unrealized mark-to-market of open positions.
Add a shared crypto symbol normalization layer that maps feed symbols to broker-tradable symbols before order submission, and use it across all bots.
Centralize order submission defaults by asset class so crypto orders automatically use supported parameters such as market/notional with time_in_force='gtc'.
Refactor Momentum to use the same resilient retry/execution path as Scalper instead of stopping after the first symbol mapping failure.
Update leaderboard/ranking logic to rank bots by correctly computed total equity or total return, not raw spent cash or faulty PnL.
Extend race export to include per-bot positions, cash, market value, unrealized PnL, realized PnL, order IDs, and final broker order statuses.
Prevent bots from scanning out-of-universe assets; Conservative should skip execution entirely when its allowed market is closed and log a concise reason.
Fix log/message truncation in UI/export so complete rationale text is preserved.
Allow multi-cycle race execution with configurable duration/interval so strategies can manage exits and not stop after a single scan.
Add explicit post-trade reconciliation after each order to verify broker acceptance, filled notional, and resulting position state.
tests_to_run: Run a crypto-only race after hours and verify Momentum and Scalper both submit tradable crypto orders successfully on first attempt without symbol-format errors.
After two BUY orders and no sells, confirm each bot shows reduced cash, nonzero open position market value, realized PnL = 0, and unrealized PnL near 0 instead of net_pnl = negative spent amount.
Verify leaderboard ranks bots by total equity/return correctly when one bot has open positions and others are in cash.
Confirm crypto orders are sent with supported Alpaca parameters, especially time_in_force='gtc', and no retry is needed for invalid TIF.
Check exported race JSON includes positions, quantities, avg entry price, market value, realized/unrealized PnL, and broker order IDs/statuses.
Verify Conservative skips crypto scans when equities are closed and logs a full non-truncated explanation.
Verify long rationale messages in live feed and export are no longer truncated.
Run a multi-cycle race and confirm bots remain active across intervals, can hold positions, and later exit positions with PnL updating correctly.
next_prompt_for_replit: Fix the most critical issue: portfolio accounting and leaderboard logic for open positions in the AI Brain 4-Bot Race. Right now a bot that buys $150 of crypto and has not sold anything is being reported as net_pnl = -150, which is wrong because spent cash is being treated as realized loss. Update the trading engine so BUY orders reduce available cash and create/update an open position with quantity, avg entry price, cost basis, and market value. Realized PnL should only change on SELLs. Unrealized PnL should be calculated from current market price minus avg entry price times quantity. Then compute per-bot total equity = cash + market value of open positions, and derive net_pnl from realized + unrealized, not from net_spent/net_received. Update the leaderboard to rank by total equity or return percentage using this corrected accounting. Also extend the race export to include for each bot: cash, position list, quantity, avg_entry_price, market_value, realized_pnl, unrealized_pnl, total_equity, and any open order status. Preserve existing bot behavior otherwise. Add/adjust tests for: 1) two BUYs and no SELLs should not produce negative realized PnL, 2) open positions should show market value and near-zero unrealized PnL immediately after fill, 3) leaderboard ordering should reflect corrected total equity. If there is already a portfolio/accounting module, patch it there rather than duplicating logic.

summary: The race barely started: only Momentum Rider and Dip Buyer produced logs, no bot completed a cycle, and no trades were recorded despite Momentum attempting crypto entries. The biggest issue is execution/state tracking failure—orders were rejected and retried, but bot metrics, cycle completion, and scan summaries never updated, while Conservative and Scalper appear completely inactive.
problems_found: Momentum Rider attempted crypto orders with an invalid time_in_force, indicating a broker/order-routing bug for crypto assets.
Momentum logs say it would retry with valid market orders, but trades remain 0, net_spent remains 0, and no order success/failure outcome is persisted.
Momentum has lastScan populated and multiple scan logs, yet cycles_completed is 0 and the system still warns it has not completed a scan cycle; scan lifecycle state is broken.
Dip Buyer produced a scan and strategy reasoning, but cycles_completed is still 0 and scan_result is null; hold/no-trade outcomes are not being stored as completed cycles.
Conservative bot is LIVE but has no lastScan, no logs, and no activity at all.
Scalper bot is LIVE but has no lastScan, no logs, and no activity at all.
Summary field bots_with_no_scan is 4, but at least Momentum and Dip Buyer did scan; summary aggregation is incorrect.
All bots have rank null with rank_label TIE, suggesting ranking logic does not handle pre-trade but partially active races correctly.
scan_result is null for every bot, so the app is not persisting structured scan decisions such as buy, hold, skipped, rejected, or error.

The AI reasoning references shared account positions already holding BTC and ETH, but exported bot/account state does not include structured existing-position data; race decisions cannot be audited reliably.
There is no explicit order event stream in the export for submitted, rejected, retried, filled, or canceled orders, creating a major observability gap.
The warning 'LIVE but has not completed a scan cycle yet' is too coarse and appears even after visible scan activity, which is misleading UX.
The race is marked active with four bots, but scheduling/orchestration appears to have only started two agents; bot runner coverage is incomplete or staggered without status visibility.
recommended_changes: Fix crypto order construction so the broker adapter uses valid parameters per asset class, especially omitting or mapping unsupported time_in_force values for crypto market/notional orders.
Implement a clear scan-cycle state machine for each bot: queued, scanning, decided, order_submitted, order_filled/order_failed, completed; increment cycles_completed even for hold/no-trade decisions.
Persist structured scan_result for every cycle with fields like action, symbols_considered, reason, strategy_constraints, order_attempted, order_status, and completion timestamp.
Add explicit order audit events to the live feed and export so retries and final execution outcomes are visible and bot metrics can reconcile with broker responses.
Fix summary aggregation so bots_with_no_scan counts only bots with no lastScan and no completed/started cycle logs.
Mark bots that have not started as STALLED, PENDING, or NOT_STARTED instead of LIVE, or ensure the scheduler immediately launches all configured bots.
Add heartbeat/startup logs for Conservative and Scalper and verify they are included in the same scheduler loop as the other bots.
Update performance metrics only after confirmed broker events, and separately track pending orders so retries do not leave trades/net_spent at zero without explanation.

Store and expose per-bot virtual allocations versus shared-account holdings to prevent confusing reasoning like 'shared account holds positions but my personal budget is unused' without a structured attribution model.
Improve warning logic to distinguish 'no scan started', 'scan in progress', 'decision made no trade', and 'order failed'.
tests_to_run: Run a 4-bot race during equity-closed hours and confirm all four bots produce startup logs and a lastScan within one scheduler interval.
Verify Momentum submits crypto orders without invalid time_in_force errors and that submitted/rejected/filled events appear in the live feed.
Force a no-trade Dip Buyer cycle and confirm cycles_completed increments to 1 with scan_result.action = HOLD or SKIP.
Confirm Conservative and Scalper no longer remain LIVE with empty logs; they should either run or show NOT_STARTED/STALLED explicitly.
Check that bots_with_no_scan in summary matches the actual number of bots with null lastScan.
Verify order retry flows update final status correctly and do not leave trades/net_spent/order state inconsistent.
Confirm export JSON now includes structured scan_result and order outcome fields for each bot.
Manually compare UI race leaderboard/rank labels against bot activity to ensure ties and inactive bots are displayed accurately.
next_prompt_for_replit: Fix the AI Brain bot cycle state tracking and crypto order execution path. Specifically: 1) update the broker order builder so crypto orders use valid Alpaca-compatible parameters and do not send unsupported time_in_force values; 2) add a structured bot cycle state machine in the AI Brain runner with statuses like NOT_STARTED, SCANNING, DECIDED, ORDER_SUBMITTED, ORDER_FILLED, ORDER_FAILED, COMPLETED; 3) ensure cycles_completed increments for every finished cycle, including HOLD/no-trade cycles; 4) persist a non-null scan_result object for each bot cycle containing action, symbols considered, reasoning summary, order_attempted, order_status, and completed_at; 5) add live feed/export events for order submitted, rejected, retried, filled, and cycle completed; 6) fix race summary aggregation so bots_with_no_scan only counts bots with no scan started. After implementation, run a local 4-bot race in crypto-only mode and verify all four bots initialize, at least one hold cycle is counted as completed, and Momentum no longer throws invalid time_in_force for crypto.

summary: The race mostly failed to start: 3 of 4 bots never began a scan, while only Momentum Rider executed two crypto buys and is temporarily shown at -$125 because open buys are being counted as negative PnL. The leaderboard and summary are therefore misleading, with inactive bots ranked above the only bot that actually traded.
problems_found: Three agents (dip_buyer, conservative, scalper) are marked LIVE but have cycles_started=0, cycle_status=NOT_STARTED, no scans, and no logs, indicating bot scheduler/launch orchestration is not starting all race participants.
Ranking logic is broken: Dip Buyer is shown rank #1 and summary leader despite zero activity, while Momentum Rider is rank #4 after being the only bot to trade.

PnL/accounting logic is incorrect or misleading: Momentum Rider shows net_pnl=-125 immediately after spending $125 on open positions with net_received=0, which suggests cash outflow is being treated as realized loss instead of open inventory/mark-to-market equity.
Race summary total_pnl=-125 is therefore also misleading because it appears to aggregate cash deployment rather than actual realized/unrealized PnL.
cycle_status for Momentum Rider is stuck at ORDER_FILLED with cycles_completed=0, suggesting cycle completion is not finalized after order submission/fill.
scan_result is null for all bots, including the bot that clearly performed a scan and made a decision; structured scan output is missing.
Symbol normalization is inconsistent: configured symbols use BTC-USD/ETH-USD, while executed/logged symbols are BTCUSD/ETHUSD. This can break position matching, deduping, analytics, and UI joins.

AI log says 'Shared account already holds both positions' while the race is supposed to compare per-bot budgets; this suggests bot reasoning is contaminated by shared account state and may cause cross-bot interference.
No position snapshot is included per bot, so there is no way to verify open holdings, entry prices, quantities, unrealized PnL, or whether the buys actually filled.
No order status/fill details are exported beyond 'Order submitted' and 'BUY', making it unclear whether these are accepted, filled, partially filled, or only simulated.
Inactive bots have only warnings in top-level errors but no root-cause diagnostics such as scheduler failure, API timeout, strategy skip, or market gating explanation.
Summary field bots_with_no_scan=3 is accurate but the app still shows active=true and all bots status=LIVE, masking degraded race health.
Latest logs are only present for Momentum Rider; missing heartbeat/startup logs for the other bots make debugging startup failures difficult.
Market-closed gating appears to route only Momentum to crypto; no evidence the other strategies have a fallback plan or explicit 'skipped because no valid market' status.
recommended_changes: Fix race orchestration so all selected bots are explicitly started when a 4-bot race begins, with per-bot startup confirmation, timeout handling, and failure reasons if a bot does not enter scanning within a defined SLA.
Correct PnL calculation to separate cash, invested capital, market value, realized PnL, unrealized PnL, and total equity; open buys should not immediately count as negative PnL.
Rework leaderboard/rank logic to rank by total equity or true total PnL, and never place NOT_STARTED bots above active bots solely because their displayed PnL is zero.
Add structured per-bot position snapshots to the export: symbol, normalized symbol, qty/notional, avg entry, last price, market value, realized PnL, unrealized PnL, and order status.
Persist structured scan_result for every cycle, including scanned universe, skipped symbols, reasons, selected candidates, and final action/no-action decision.
Standardize symbol formatting across config, logs, orders, positions, and analytics using a single canonical format plus broker symbol mapping utilities.
Isolate bot race accounting from shared account holdings so each bot reasons from its own virtual portfolio state, not global account positions.
Add explicit statuses for skipped/ineligible cycles such as MARKET_CLOSED_NO_ELIGIBLE_SYMBOLS, STARTUP_FAILED, SCAN_COMPLETED_NO_ACTION, and CYCLE_COMPLETED.
Expose startup and scheduler diagnostics in latest_logs for every bot: race assigned, cycle launched, market gate result, scan start, scan finish, and any exception stack/message.
Update summary generation to report race health metrics such as started_bots, failed_bots, skipped_bots, open_positions, realized_pnl, unrealized_pnl, and cash_remaining.
Add a guard so active=true is not shown as healthy race state when most bots have not started; surface a degraded state/banner when startup coverage is incomplete.
Ensure cycles_completed increments after a full scan/order decision lifecycle, even when no trade is made.
tests_to_run: Start a fresh 4-bot race after market close and verify all 4 bots leave NOT_STARTED within the expected startup window.
Verify each bot writes startup logs, scan start logs, and either scan_result or an explicit skip/failure reason.
Confirm ranking places active/trading bots correctly and does not rank idle zero-scan bots above them by default.
Execute a buy in paper mode and verify net_pnl does not drop negative solely because cash was deployed; check realized vs unrealized vs equity fields.
Verify race summary totals match per-bot aggregates for cash, market value, realized PnL, unrealized PnL, and total equity.
Check that BTC-USD/ETH-USD are represented consistently across watchlist, logs, orders, positions, and exported symbols.
Verify Momentum can trade crypto without referencing shared account positions from other bots.
Run a scenario where a bot has no valid trades and ensure cycle_status becomes SCAN_COMPLETED_NO_ACTION with cycles_completed incremented.
Simulate scheduler failure for one bot and verify the export includes a concrete startup failure diagnostic instead of only 'has not started its first scan yet'.
Inspect export JSON and confirm each bot now includes position snapshots, structured scan_result, and order/fill status fields.
next_prompt_for_replit: Fix the AI Brain 4-bot race startup/orchestration bug first. Right now only Momentum Rider starts, while Dip Buyer, Conservative, and Scalper remain LIVE but NOT_STARTED with zero scans. I want you to trace the race initialization flow end-to-end and make sure every selected bot is launched reliably at race start. Implement: (1) explicit per-bot startup dispatch when a race is created, (2) a startup timeout/heartbeat so a bot that fails to begin scanning within the SLA is marked STARTUP_FAILED with a reason, (3) startup logs for every bot such as 'race assigned', 'cycle #1 queued', 'scan started', and any caught exception, (4) export support so the race JSON includes meaningful startup diagnostics instead of only generic warnings, and (5) a degraded race health status when not all bots start. Also ensure bots that cannot trade because of market conditions still enter a completed cycle state with a structured skip reason rather than staying NOT_STARTED. After coding, run a local test of a fresh 4-bot race and show me the before/after exported JSON for bot startup fields, cycle_status, cycles_started, latest_logs, and errors.

summary: All four bots completed one scan in an after-hours session where only BTC and ETH were tradable. Momentum Rider and Scalper both bought the same two crypto assets, while Dip Buyer and Conservative held cash; the race standings are misleading because open positions are being counted as losses and idle bots rank above active bots.
problems_found: PnL/accounting bug: Momentum Rider shows net_pnl -125 with net_spent 125 and net_received 0, and Scalper shows net_pnl -150 with net_spent 150 and net_received 0, which implies buys are treated as realized losses instead of open positions with unrealized PnL.
Ranking bug: Dip Buyer is ranked #1 and Conservative #2 solely because they stayed in cash, while bots with filled positions are ranked lower due to the accounting error.
Shared-account contamination: multiple bots explicitly note the shared account already holds BTC and ETH, meaning race bots are not truly isolated and can influence each other's exposure/state.
Missing holdings data per bot: export has no per-bot open positions, entry prices, quantities, mark prices, unrealized PnL, or reserved cash, so performance cannot be audited.
Missing execution details: filled orders lack order IDs, fill prices, filled quantities, fees/slippage, and broker confirmation status.
Missing portfolio metrics: no cash remaining, equity value, exposure by symbol, or realized vs unrealized PnL breakdown per bot.
Strategy duplication/low differentiation: Momentum Rider and Scalper both bought BTC and ETH off the same positive momentum signal, reducing race diversity and making comparison less useful.
Universe/config mismatch: Conservative references AMZN and NVDA as allowed symbols, but those symbols are not in the race symbol list.
Truncated UI/log text: several reasoning_summary and info messages are cut off mid-sentence, suggesting export/UI clipping.
Volume data inconsistency: BTC volume appears as 258k in one bot log and 36860k in another within minutes, indicating inconsistent normalization or source formatting.
No error reporting for skipped validations: bots say they will confirm account/positions/market data in parallel, but export contains no explicit validation results.
No stop-loss/take-profit state tracking: Scalper mentions stop -1% and take profit +1.5%, but there are no attached protective order records or monitoring state in the export.
recommended_changes: Fix race accounting first: treat BUY orders as conversion of cash into open positions, not negative PnL; compute net liquidation value as cash + market value of open holdings and derive realized/unrealized/total PnL correctly.
Add isolated virtual sub-portfolios per bot so each agent tracks its own cash, positions, cost basis, and exits independent of the shared brokerage account.
Expose per-bot positions in the export and UI: symbol, qty/notional, avg entry, current mark, market value, unrealized PnL, realized PnL, and cash remaining.
Update ranking logic to sort by total equity or total PnL percentage, not by flawed net_pnl derived from spend/receive flows.
Persist full execution metadata for each order: client order ID, broker order ID, submitted/filled timestamps, fill price, quantity, notional, fees, and order status lifecycle.
Normalize market data formatting across bots, especially volume units, and include the source timestamp used for each decision.
Align strategy config with race symbols: either include AMZN/NVDA in the race universe or remove them from Conservative's allowed list.
Improve agent differentiation by adding anti-correlation or overlap penalties so Momentum and Scalper do not always clone the same BTC/ETH trades.
Stop truncating reasoning/log strings in exports and ensure full text is preserved even if UI shows a shortened preview.
Record validation checkpoints explicitly in logs/export: market open/closed result, account fetch success, positions fetch success, and data freshness.
Track and export protective exit logic state for strategies that declare stop-loss/take-profit rules.
tests_to_run: Run a fresh 4-bot race after market close and verify that a BUY decreases cash, creates an open position, and does not immediately create equal negative PnL.
Confirm Momentum Rider and Scalper show open BTC/ETH positions with entry price, current value, unrealized PnL, and correct remaining cash.
Verify leaderboard ordering changes based on total equity/PnL rather than favoring bots that never trade.
Check that two bots trading the same symbol do not share or overwrite each other's position state in the race portfolio model.
Inspect export JSON to confirm full non-truncated reasoning_summary and info messages are preserved.
Verify Conservative's allowed universe matches the configured symbols shown in the race.
Compare BTC/ETH volume values across all bot logs in the same cycle and confirm consistent formatting/source.
Place a trade and verify order metadata includes fill price, quantity, timestamps, and broker/order IDs in the export.
If stop-loss/take-profit is enabled for Scalper, verify those protective rules are persisted and visible in bot state/export.
next_prompt_for_replit: Fix the AI Brain race portfolio accounting and ranking so buys are no longer counted as immediate losses. Implement isolated virtual sub-portfolios per bot with these fields: starting_budget, cash, open_positions[], realized_pnl, unrealized_pnl, total_pnl, total_equity. On BUY, reduce bot cash and create/update an open position with symbol, qty/notional, avg_entry_price, cost_basis, opened_at. On SELL, increase cash, reduce/close position, and move PnL into realized_pnl. Add mark-to-market valuation using latest prices so unrealized_pnl and total_equity update every cycle. Update leaderboard ranking to sort by total_equity (or total_pnl_pct) instead of current net_pnl. Extend the race export schema to include per-bot cash, positions, realized/unrealized/total PnL, total equity, and execution details. Keep existing broker paper trades if needed, but race scoring must come from the isolated per-bot virtual portfolio, not shared account balances. Also add a regression test covering this exact case: a bot buys $125 of crypto in cycle 1 and should show cash=125, open_position_value near 125, total_pnl near 0 immediately after fill, not -125.

summary: The race mostly ran as expected in a crypto-only after-hours environment: Momentum Rider and Scalper took BTC/ETH exposure while Dip Buyer and Conservative correctly stayed in cash. Performance differences were tiny, but the export shows clear strategy-rule inconsistencies, truncated reasoning/log data, and weak race design when only two crypto symbols are available.
problems_found: Scalper violated its own stated max position rule: it says max position is ~30% of $250 budget (~$75), but ended with BTC notional of $175 and still justified the trade as valid.
Scalper's cycle-3 AI reasoning is internally contradictory: it says current positions are already at budget caps and 'likely hold', then immediately buys more BTC.

Execution detail math for Scalper cycle 1 is inconsistent: first BTC buy notional is $75 at fill_price 80234.4, but qty 0.0009347611498309953 implies about $75, while final aggregated avg_entry_price/cost basis later suggests multiple BTC fills were merged without a clear per-lot model.

Symbol formatting is inconsistent across the export: inputs use BTC-USD/ETH-USD while positions and trades use BTC/USD and ETH/USD, which can break joins, rankings, chart lookups, and PnL reconciliation.
AI reasoning summaries and multiple log messages are truncated mid-sentence, indicating export/UI clipping or storage length limits that hide decision context.

Market volume data is inconsistent between scans for the same symbols over a few minutes: BTC volume shows 2470k in some logs and 176k in later logs; ETH shows 10473k vs 223k, suggesting unit/normalization bugs.
Race design is weak in crypto-only mode because Dip Buyer and Conservative had no realistic chance to act, so the 4-bot comparison was effectively reduced to 2 active bots.
Conservative strategy references symbols not present in the configured symbol list (AMZN, NVDA), indicating mismatch between strategy prompt/universe and app-provided tradable symbols.
No sell activity occurred and there is no evidence of exit rule validation, so stop-loss/take-profit logic was not exercised or observable in this run.
There is missing explicit per-cycle market snapshot data in structured form; only human-readable logs exist, making audit/debugging harder.
There is missing structured rejection/constraint metadata showing why candidate trades were blocked; bots only provide narrative text instead of machine-checkable rule evaluations.
The app reports no errors even though strategy-rule violations occurred, implying missing validation/guardrails before order placement.
recommended_changes: Add a hard pre-trade risk/constraint validator that enforces per-symbol max allocation, total exposure limits, allowed universe, and mode-specific restrictions before any order is submitted; reject and log violations in structured form.
Normalize symbols to a single canonical format everywhere (prefer BTC-USD/ETH-USD or a central mapper) and use it consistently in market data, positions, execution records, and logs.
Store and export full AI reasoning/log text without truncation, or add separate full_text fields so decision traces remain auditable.
Standardize market volume units and include raw volume plus display_volume/unit metadata to prevent 176k vs 2470k inconsistencies.
Add structured cycle diagnostics per bot: candidate symbols, rule checks, thresholds, reasons blocked, portfolio caps, and final decision.
Update strategy prompts/config so each bot only references symbols actually in the provided universe, or dynamically inject the allowed universe into every bot prompt.

Improve race fairness by either pausing incompatible bots in crypto-only sessions, switching them to standby, or expanding the after-hours universe so Dip Buyer and Conservative can participate meaningfully.
Add post-trade invariant checks that compare bot-declared strategy limits against resulting holdings and flag impossible states in errors/warnings.
Track positions by lots or clearly document aggregation logic so avg_entry_price, qty, and cost_basis are always reproducible.
Add dedicated tests for multi-cycle accumulation behavior to ensure 'max position' limits apply to aggregate exposure, not just single order size.
tests_to_run: Run a new 4-bot crypto-only race and confirm Scalper cannot exceed its configured per-symbol max allocation across multiple cycles.
Verify that any blocked trade now produces a structured rejection reason in the export and optionally surfaces in the live feed.
Confirm BTC/ETH symbols appear identically in symbols list, market snapshots, open positions, execution details, and live feed entries.
Check that full reasoning_summary and latest_logs messages are preserved end-to-end without clipping in export and UI.
Validate volume display consistency for BTC and ETH across consecutive scans, including raw value and formatted unit labels.
Confirm Conservative and Dip Buyer either enter standby/inactive-compatible mode during crypto-only sessions or clearly show they are excluded from ranking fairness calculations.
Replay a scenario where stop-loss or take-profit should trigger and verify sell execution, realized PnL, and logs are correct.
Audit one bot's aggregated position after multiple buys to ensure qty, avg_entry_price, cost_basis, and market_value reconcile exactly.
next_prompt_for_replit: Implement a hard pre-trade risk validator for AI Brain paper trading and wire it into the bot execution flow before any order is placed. The top goal is to stop strategy-rule violations like the Scalper exceeding its stated max per-symbol allocation. Add a reusable validator module that checks: allowed symbol universe, market session restrictions, available cash, max order notional, aggregate per-symbol exposure cap, aggregate total exposure cap, and any bot-specific strategy limits already defined in config. The validator must run before placePaperOrder and return a structured result like { allowed, violations: [...], computedExposure: {...} }. If validation fails, do not place the order; instead log a structured rejection event and include it in the race export under the bot's cycle diagnostics. Also add post-trade invariant checks so any impossible state is surfaced in errors/warnings. While doing this, normalize symbols through one shared utility so BTC-USD and BTC/USD cannot mismatch during validation. Finally, add/update tests covering: (1) Scalper cannot accumulate BTC above its max allocation across multiple cycles, (2) valid orders still execute normally, and (3) rejected orders appear in export/logs with explicit violation reasons.

summary: Only Momentum Rider completed a full cycle and opened two crypto positions while the market was closed; the other three bots did not meaningfully participate, so the race result is mostly an initialization/status failure rather than a strategy comparison.
problems_found: Race fairness failure: only 1 of 4 bots completed a scan, with Conservative and Scalper still at NOT_STARTED and Dip Buyer stuck at SCANNING.
Likely scheduler/orchestration bug: bots are marked LIVE but two never started and one never finished within the export window.
Ranking is misleading because all bots have identical total_equity=250 and rank is effectively arbitrary/insertion-based while only one bot actually traded.
Dip Buyer has cycles_started=1 but cycles_completed=0 and scan_result=null, indicating a hung, timed-out, or unpersisted scan state.
Conservative and Scalper have no last_scan, no logs, and cycles_started=0 despite being in a 4-Bot Race, suggesting race kickoff did not fan out to all agents.
Logs and reasoning messages are truncated in multiple places, reducing observability and making it hard to audit decisions.
Symbol normalization is inconsistent: top-level symbols and active_scan_symbols use BTC-USD/ETH-USD while positions and executions use BTC/USD and ETH/USD.
scan_result for Momentum says action=BUY and order_status=filled but does not capture per-symbol outcomes even though two separate buys occurred.
No explicit timeout/error record exists for the stalled bots; warnings only mention not started, not why they failed.
total_pnl remains 0 immediately after fills because last_price equals fill_price, so current leaderboard gives no signal of strategy quality.
Missing market-data snapshot fields per bot (price change %, volume, qualification/rejection reasons) in structured form; these only appear in free-text logs.
No evidence that non-participating bots adapted their strategy universe for market_open=false; there is no structured record of skipped symbols or why no trades were made.
recommended_changes: Fix race orchestration so all bots are launched concurrently at race start, each receives the same active symbol universe, and each scan is guaranteed to transition to COMPLETED, NO_ACTION, FAILED, or TIMED_OUT.
Add per-bot scan timeout handling with persisted failure state, error message, and latest_logs entry so bots cannot remain indefinitely in SCANNING.
Normalize symbols across the entire stack to a single canonical format (prefer BTC-USD/ETH-USD) for watchlists, orders, positions, logs, and execution records.
Change ranking logic to deprioritize bots that have not completed a cycle and display a 'race incomplete' state when fewer than all bots finish their first scan.
Expand scan_result schema to include symbols_evaluated, symbols_qualified, symbols_rejected, per-symbol reason codes, and array of orders_attempted/orders_filled rather than a single action field.
Persist full, untruncated AI reasoning and full user-visible logs, or add separate structured fields plus a UI-safe preview so exports remain audit-friendly.
Add explicit startup diagnostics for each bot: kickoff timestamp, strategy init status, symbol universe assigned, data fetch status, and completion timestamp.
Ensure every strategy handles market_open=false deterministically, either by scanning crypto-only or returning a structured NO_ACTION with reason.
Add export-level health summary metrics such as bots_completed_first_cycle, bots_failed, bots_timed_out, and race_ready_for_comparison.
Add a refresh/revaluation step after orders fill so unrealized PnL and leaderboard can reflect live price movement when available.
tests_to_run: Start a new 4-Bot Race with market_open=false and verify all four bots enter scanning within seconds and all exit to COMPLETED, NO_ACTION, FAILED, or TIMED_OUT.
Verify Dip Buyer, Conservative, and Scalper each produce scan_result data and latest_logs even when they place no trades.
Confirm no bot remains stuck in SCANNING after the configured timeout and that timeout errors appear in export.errors and bot.latest_logs.
Verify symbol format is identical everywhere in export output, live feed, positions, and execution_details.
Check ranking behavior when some bots do not finish: UI/export should mark race incomplete instead of implying a valid winner.
Run a market-closed scenario and confirm each strategy gets the crypto-only universe or emits a structured NO_ACTION reason for skipping.
Verify Momentum scan_result now records both BTC and ETH orders separately rather than one generic BUY result.
Confirm logs and reasoning are no longer truncated in exports.
After order fills, verify a reprice step updates unrealized_pnl when market data changes.
next_prompt_for_replit: Fix the AI Brain 4-Bot Race orchestration so every bot reliably participates and no bot can remain stuck in SCANNING. Implement this in the race runner/orchestrator layer first. Requirements: 1) when a race starts, launch all configured bots concurrently with the same race context and assigned symbol universe; 2) every bot cycle must end in one of these terminal states: COMPLETED, NO_ACTION, FAILED, or TIMED_OUT; 3) add a per-bot timeout (for example 20-30s) that converts lingering SCANNING cycles into TIMED_OUT, persists a structured scan_result/error object, and appends a latest_logs entry explaining the timeout; 4) add startup diagnostics to each bot state such as started_at, completed_at, data_fetch_started, data_fetch_completed, strategy_started, strategy_completed, and any init error; 5) ensure market_open=false is passed into every strategy and that bots that choose not to trade still return a structured NO_ACTION result instead of null; 6) update the export payload and types so race health includes bots_completed_first_cycle, bots_failed, bots_timed_out, and race_ready_for_comparison; 7) adjust ranking/UI logic to show race incomplete when not all bots have reached a terminal first-cycle state. Also add unit/integration coverage for: all 4 bots start, one bot timeout path, one bot no-action path, and export state after incomplete race. Keep changes minimal but robust, and summarize all modified files.

summary: The race completed cleanly, but it produced a misleading result: two bots stayed entirely in cash and ranked above the only active bots because the market was closed for equities and only BTC/ETH were tradable. Momentum and Scalper opened crypto positions early, drifted slightly negative, and Momentum also exposed a strategy/risk-engine mismatch by attempting add-on orders that were guaranteed to be rejected.
problems_found: Ranking logic is misleading: Dip Buyer is shown as leader at 0.00% PnL simply because inactive cash preservation beat small unrealized losses from active bots.
Race comparison is low quality because the session was effectively crypto-only while two strategies are equity-only or equity-preferred, so the race is not a fair apples-to-apples comparison.

Momentum bot generated an invalid plan: its AI rationale explicitly said adding $31.25 to BTC and ETH would remain compliant, but the risk engine correctly rejected both orders due to the 25% per-symbol cap.

There is a pre-trade validation gap: the strategy proposed orders that violated known portfolio/risk constraints instead of sizing to remaining allowable exposure or skipping the order before submission.
Symbol format is inconsistent across the export: watchlist/market data uses BTC-USD and ETH-USD, while positions/execution/errors use BTC/USD and ETH/USD.
Conservative strategy references symbols not present in the race universe (AMZN, NVDA), indicating strategy universe and race symbol list are out of sync.
bots_completed is 1 while all 4 bots show completed first cycle and no bot failed/timed out; this status field appears semantically incorrect or stale.
first_cycle_terminal is true for all bots despite cycles_completed being 4, which suggests a lifecycle flag is misnamed or incorrectly persisted.
Logs and reasoning summaries are truncated in multiple places, reducing debuggability and making it harder to audit decision quality.
Volume data appears inconsistent between cycles (e.g. ~11391k then ~75547k) without metadata on source, interval, or normalization, limiting trust in market context.
No benchmark or 'cash-adjusted/participation-adjusted' metric is provided, so inactive bots can dominate rankings during closed-market sessions.
No explicit race-level warning flags that most bots had no actionable universe due to market-hours filtering, even though race_ready_for_comparison is true.
recommended_changes: Add strategy-aware pre-trade risk validation and order sizing so bots calculate remaining allowable exposure before proposing or submitting orders; if cap is reached, they should log HOLD/NO_ACTION instead of triggering blocked orders.

Update leaderboard/ranking to distinguish inactive cash preservation from active performance, e.g. add 'participation-aware rank', 'cash rank', or require a minimum trade/action threshold before declaring a leader.

Add race viability checks before starting or before marking race_ready_for_comparison=true; if the current market session leaves most bots with no tradable universe, show a warning and optionally defer the race until a compatible session.
Normalize symbol formatting across all layers to one canonical representation, preferably BTC-USD/ETH-USD, with adapters only at broker API boundaries.

Align each bot's declared strategy universe with the actual race symbols, or dynamically inject only symbols the bot can truly trade so Conservative does not reason about AMZN/NVDA when they are absent.
Fix race_health/status semantics so bots_completed, cycle_status, and first_cycle_terminal accurately reflect multi-cycle execution.
Store full, non-truncated reasoning_summary and feed log messages in export/debug mode.
Add benchmark fields such as buy-and-hold BTC/ETH and 'percent of capital deployed' to make no-trade outcomes easier to interpret.
Add an explicit 'blocked_order_count' and 'precheck_prevented_order_count' per bot to separate smart holds from preventable execution mistakes.
tests_to_run: Run a crypto-only closed-equity session and verify Momentum no longer attempts add-on orders when current exposure is already at the per-symbol cap.
Verify Momentum instead logs a compliant HOLD/NO_ACTION decision with explicit remaining allowable exposure = 0 for BTC and ETH.
Open a session where partial remaining exposure exists and confirm order sizing is clipped to the exact allowed amount rather than rejected.
Confirm all exports, positions, execution details, errors, and UI cards use the same canonical symbol format.
Verify leaderboard behavior when some bots make no trades and others have small unrealized losses; ensure the UI clearly distinguishes inactive/cash leaders from active strategy leaders.
Check race readiness messaging when market-hours filtering leaves most bots without an actionable universe.
Validate bots_completed, cycle_status, and first_cycle_terminal fields across 1-cycle and multi-cycle races.
Inspect exported logs to confirm reasoning_summary and feed messages are no longer truncated in debug/export mode.
next_prompt_for_replit: Implement pre-trade risk-aware order planning for AI Brain bots, starting with Momentum Rider. Right now the bot can propose add-on orders that the risk engine later rejects, as seen when Momentum tried to add $31.25 to BTC/USD and ETH/USD despite already being at the 25% per-symbol cap. I want the strategy layer to know the remaining allowable exposure before it generates orders. Please: 1) add a helper in the portfolio/risk layer that returns remaining allowable notional per symbol based on current positions, budget, max per-symbol exposure, cash, and any existing pending orders; 2) update the momentum strategy to use that helper so it never proposes an order above the allowed size, and if remaining capacity is zero it should output HOLD/NO_ACTION with a clear explanation instead of attempting a blocked order; 3) if remaining capacity is positive but smaller than desired order size, clip the order down to the maximum allowed amount; 4) keep the final risk engine validation in place as a safety net, but the normal path should avoid rejections; 5) add unit tests for zero remaining capacity, partial remaining capacity, and exact-capacity cases; 6) update export/log output so we can see desired size, allowed size, and final submitted size in the bot reasoning. Also normalize symbol handling in this flow so BTC-USD/BTC/USD mismatches do not break exposure calculations.

summary: All 4 bots completed their first scan without system errors, but only Momentum Rider and Scalper placed trades because the market was closed and only crypto was eligible. The race comparison is effectively meaningless so far because every bot has 0 PnL, ranking is based on no realized performance, and two bots were structurally unable to act in the available market regime.
problems_found: Ranking is misleading: Momentum Rider is #1 and Scalper is #4 despite both having identical total_equity and total_pnl of 0, suggesting unstable or arbitrary tie-breaking.
Race is marked ready_for_comparison after only one cycle with zero PnL across all bots, so the comparison state is premature.
Conservative is effectively handicapped at night because its strategy universe is equities-only, making it non-competitive whenever the race starts during closed equity hours.
Dip Buyer was not broken, but it had no eligible setup because only BTC and ETH were available and both were green; this makes the race mode highly sensitive to time-of-day and symbol availability.
Open positions show last_price equal to avg_entry_price immediately after fills, so there is no post-trade mark-to-market refresh and unrealized PnL cannot differentiate active bots yet.

Inconsistent symbol formatting between input symbols (BTC-USD, ETH-USD) and executed/open position symbols (BTC/USD, ETH/USD) can create matching, deduplication, charting, and portfolio reconciliation bugs.

Market data is fetched separately per bot, producing slightly different quoted prices for the same asset at nearly the same timestamp (e.g. BTC-USD 79906.05 vs 79910.01), which undermines fairness in a race comparison.
Reasoning summaries and log messages are truncated in export fields, which loses important audit context for why decisions were made.

Conservative references AMZN and NVDA in its strategy universe, but those symbols are not present in the configured race symbols, indicating a mismatch between strategy prompt/universe and race configuration.
first_cycle_terminal is true for all bots, including LIVE bots with open positions, implying confusing lifecycle semantics or a bug in bot state naming.
bots_completed is 2 and bots_no_action is 2 while all bot statuses remain LIVE; status model appears inconsistent between cycle completion and ongoing race state.
There is no explicit fairness metadata showing whether all bots used the exact same market snapshot, timestamp, and price source for decisions and fills.
No exit-management evidence is present yet for bots that opened positions, so stop-loss/take-profit logic may not be verifiable from the current export.
No benchmark comparison or holdout baseline is included, so it is hard to tell whether no-action bots are prudent or simply inactive.
recommended_changes: Fix race ranking and comparison gating so bots are not meaningfully ranked until there is non-zero PnL, multiple cycles, or a deterministic tie-break policy based on documented rules.
Normalize symbols across the entire app to one canonical format internally (prefer BTC-USD/ETH-USD) and only transform at broker API boundaries.
Use a single shared market snapshot per cycle for all bots so every strategy sees identical prices, percent changes, and volumes before making decisions.
Add a mark-to-market refresh immediately after order execution and before export so open positions reflect current last_price and unrealized_pnl instead of entry-price placeholders.

Make race mode regime-aware: either start races only when enough symbols are tradable for all bots, or adapt/disable bots whose universe is unavailable and label them as ineligible rather than weak performers.
Align each bot's declared strategy universe with the configured race symbols; remove references to AMZN/NVDA unless they are actually included or fetch them explicitly.
Clarify lifecycle fields by separating bot session status from cycle status; avoid setting first_cycle_terminal for bots that remain active with open positions.
Expand export payloads to include full, untruncated reasoning, a tie_break_reason field, snapshot_id, decision_price_timestamp, and fill_source metadata.
Add fairness diagnostics to the export showing shared snapshot timestamp, per-bot decision latency, and whether fills used snapshot price or live broker quote.
Improve UI labeling so NO_ACTION due to unavailable universe is shown differently from NO_ACTION due to strategy rejection.
Add baseline comparison metrics such as cash-only, equal-weight BTC/ETH, and buy-and-hold for active symbols.
Introduce manual or scheduled multi-cycle replay tests so momentum and scalper exit logic can be observed and compared over time, not just on entry.
tests_to_run: Run a night-time crypto-only race and verify all bots either use the same snapshot or are explicitly marked ineligible; rankings should remain tied if equity and PnL are identical.
Run a market-hours race with equities open and confirm Conservative can actually evaluate only configured symbols and no longer references missing AMZN/NVDA unless included.
Verify BTC-USD and ETH-USD remain canonical in scans, orders, positions, logs, exports, and portfolio matching, with any broker-specific BTC/USD translation hidden internally.
Place trades and confirm last_price and unrealized_pnl refresh after fills instead of staying equal to avg_entry_price by default.
Verify race_ready_for_comparison stays false until the new comparison threshold is met, and confirm tie_break_reason appears in export when ranks differ.
Check that bots with identical equity/PnL get identical rank treatment or deterministic documented ordering.
Inspect AI reasoning and scan_result fields in the export to confirm full text is preserved without truncation.
Confirm LIVE status, cycle_status, and first_cycle_terminal semantics are internally consistent for bots with open positions and for no-action bots.
next_prompt_for_replit: Implement the race fairness and ranking fix for AI Brain 4-Bot Race. Specifically: 1) create a shared per-cycle market snapshot service so all bots read the exact same prices, percent changes, volume, and market-open/closed status for a cycle; include a snapshot_id and snapshot_timestamp in bot diagnostics and export. 2) update ranking logic so bots are not arbitrarily ranked when total_equity and total_pnl are identical; use deterministic tie handling and expose tie_break_reason. If all bots are tied on comparison metrics, mark the race as tied and avoid misleading #1/#4 ordering. 3) update race_ready_for_comparison gating so a race is only considered ready when there is meaningful comparison data, such as at least one completed cycle for all bots plus non-zero PnL, or at minimum a documented fallback rule if we still want post-cycle comparison. 4) preserve backward compatibility in UI but add fields needed in export: snapshot_id, snapshot_timestamp, tie_break_reason, comparison_metrics_used. 5) add tests covering: identical PnL/equity tie case, crypto-only night race with shared snapshot, and deterministic ranking output. Also clean up any UI labels in RaceResults so tied bots are shown clearly instead of fake winners.

summary: The race completed its first cycle cleanly with all 4 bots scanning, but only Momentum Rider and Scalper placed trades because the market was closed and only BTC/ETH were eligible. The result is not meaningfully comparable yet because rankings were assigned while all bots still have identical total PnL/equity and there are inconsistencies in symbol handling and market data snapshots.
problems_found: Ranking logic appears wrong or misleading: all bots have total_equity 250 and total_pnl 0, yet they are ranked #1 through #4 with Momentum first and Scalper last.
Scalper and Momentum are effectively duplicative in this cycle: both are momentum-chasing crypto buyers, reducing strategy diversity in a 4-bot race.

Dip Buyer and Conservative were structurally handicapped because after-hours filtering left only BTC-USD and ETH-USD, which do not fit their entry rules; this creates dead cycles and weak comparison quality.
Symbol normalization is inconsistent across the export: configured symbols use BTC-USD/ETH-USD while positions and fills use BTC/USD and ETH/USD.
Market data is inconsistent across bots in the same race at nearly the same timestamp: BTC is shown as 79846.77 for most bots but 79835.32 for Scalper, and volume differs sharply as well.
Conservative bot reasoning references symbols not present in the race universe (AMZN, NVDA), suggesting strategy text or universe config is out of sync with the actual race symbols.
No mark-to-market update occurred after order fills, so open positions show last_price equal to entry and unrealized_pnl remains 0; this makes early race comparison low-value.
The app marks race_ready_for_comparison true after only first-cycle completion even though 2 bots are still status LIVE and only 2 are counted as bots_completed.
bots_completed=2 while all four bots have first_cycle_terminal=true and cycle_status finalized; completion semantics are unclear or buggy.
Logs and reasoning summaries are truncated in export fields, which removes useful debugging context for strategy decisions.
No explicit per-bot performance basis is included for ranking decisions such as net exposure, risk-adjusted score, idle cash penalty, or tie-break rule.
There is no indication whether non-trading bots were prevented from considering alternative assets or fallback logic when their strategy universe became unavailable.
recommended_changes: Fix race ranking and comparison gating so bots are not assigned ordered ranks unless there is a real differentiator such as marked-to-market equity, realized PnL, or an explicit deterministic tie rule; show ties when equity is equal.
Add a post-trade portfolio repricing step using one shared market snapshot for all bots in a cycle, so unrealized PnL and total_equity reflect current prices immediately after execution.
Centralize symbol normalization so all layers use one canonical form and map display symbols separately; eliminate BTC-USD vs BTC/USD mismatches in positions, logs, and execution details.
Use a single shared quote snapshot per race cycle for all bots instead of fetching per bot independently, preventing inconsistent prices/volumes and unfair comparisons.
Make race_ready_for_comparison depend on all bots reaching a consistent terminal state for the cycle and align bots_completed semantics with first_cycle_terminal/cycle_status.
Increase strategy diversity by giving Scalper a genuinely different micro-timeframe or quick-exit behavior, or replace it with a different style bot when only crypto is tradable.
Add fallback logic for Dip Buyer and Conservative when the normal universe is unavailable, such as adaptive thresholds for crypto or a 'sit out but not penalized' classification.
Synchronize each bot's declared universe and reasoning text with the actual configured race symbols so bots do not mention unavailable names like AMZN and NVDA.
Expand export diagnostics to include ranking_basis, tie_break_reason, data_snapshot_id, normalized_symbol_map, and whether a bot was universe-constrained.
Stop truncating reasoning_summary/latest_logs in exports, or include full_text fields for debugging and auditability.
tests_to_run: Run a 4-bot race after market close and verify all bots use the exact same BTC/ETH quote snapshot values and snapshot ID.
Verify that when all bots have equal total_equity/total_pnl, the UI/export shows a tie or neutral ordering instead of arbitrary #1-#4 ranking.
Place crypto buys and confirm positions, execution_details, logs, and export all use the same canonical symbol format.
Confirm unrealized_pnl and total_equity update immediately after fills using the shared post-trade repricing snapshot.
Verify race_ready_for_comparison stays false until every bot reaches the same cycle terminal state and bots_completed count matches visible statuses.
Check Conservative bot no longer references AMZN/NVDA unless they are actually in the race universe.
Run an after-hours race and confirm Dip Buyer/Conservative are labeled as universe-constrained or use documented fallback logic rather than appearing simply weak.
Inspect exported reasoning_summary and latest_logs to confirm full text is preserved or an untruncated field is available.
next_prompt_for_replit: Fix the AI Brain race ranking/comparison pipeline so results are fair and deterministic. Implement the top change by updating the race engine and ranking logic to: 1) use a single shared market snapshot per cycle for all bots, 2) reprice all open positions after executions using that same snapshot, and 3) compute ranks from marked-to-market total_equity with proper tie handling. If multiple bots have equal total_equity within a tiny epsilon, mark them tied and do not assign misleading ordered ranks. Also make race_ready_for_comparison true only when every bot has reached a terminal state for the cycle and bots_completed semantics are consistent. Add export fields for ranking_basis, tie_break_reason, snapshot_id, and whether ranks are tied. While doing this, normalize symbols consistently so BTC-USD and BTC/USD cannot diverge across positions, fills, logs, and exports. Please update the relevant ranking, race engine, portfolio repricing, market snapshot, and export files, and include focused tests or validation code for equal-equity ties, shared snapshot usage, and post-trade repricing.
